title: hkuds-cli-anything
summary: This repo understands that the next wave of software users will be AI agents, not humans.  It provides a systematic methodology (HARNESS.
creator_view: This repo understands that the next wave of software users will be AI agents, not humans. It provides a systematic methodology (HARNESS.md) for wrapping any desktop application in a CLI that agents can invoke. The 7-phase SOP, SKILL.md generation, and CLI-Hub registry represent a complete ecosystem for agent-native software — directly relevant to JASON-OS's vision of AI-operated workflows.

candidates.name: HARNESS.md 7-Phase SOP
candidates.description: Systematic methodology for wrapping GUI apps as agent-native CLIs. Directly applicable to JASON-OS Domain 02a.

candidates.name: SKILL.md Command Catalog Format
candidates.description: 37 SKILL.md files. Flat command references vs sonash workflow definitions. Design space comparison for skill format evolution.

candidates.name: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
candidates.description: First public Claude Code plugin format reference. Compare against sonash .claude-plugin/ structure.

candidates.name: Registry + CLI-Hub Distribution
candidates.description: registry.json (35 CLIs) → static hub → pip install. Agent-discoverable via meta-skill. Model for JASON-OS skill distribution.

candidates.name: ReplSkin Terminal UI
candidates.description: Python prompt_toolkit branded REPL. Pattern portable, code Python-specific. Reference for JASON-OS interactive modes.

candidates.name: Codec Allowlist Pattern
candidates.description: frozenset-based subprocess arg validation. Similar to existing sonash security patterns.

candidates.name: Skill Generator (Jinja2)
candidates.description: Auto-generates SKILL.md from CLI introspection. Pattern portable to Node/TS.

candidates.name: Agent-Native Software Methodology
candidates.description: Core insight: wrap GUI software systematically for agents. HARNESS.md is the how-to. Foundational for JASON-OS Domain 02a.

candidates.name: Build-vs-Integrate Decision for JASON-OS
candidates.description: Should JASON-OS invoke CLI-Anything harnesses (Python) or generate Node/TS wrappers using same methodology? Critical architecture decision.

candidates.name: Quality Treadmill Case Study
candidates.description: 28K stars in 29d, 35 harnesses, 0 CI enforcement. Growth outpacing gates. Opposite of build-your-own-x stagnation.

candidates.name: MCP Backend Pattern
candidates.description: guides/mcp-backend.md. Wrap MCP servers as CLI backends via ClientSession + stdio_client. You have 3 MCP servers. Directly applicable.

candidates.name: Skill Auto-Generation Guide
candidates.description: guides/skill-generation.md. Introspect Click decorators → Jinja2 → SKILL.md. Could inform /skill-creator automation.

candidates.name: Mermaid Harness
candidates.description: Lightest-weight harness. No local binary (mermaid.ink cloud). Ready to use for agent-driven diagrams.

candidates.name: Exa Harness
candidates.description: AI search + structured extraction via Exa API. Alternative/complement for /deep-research searchers.

candidates.name: No-CI-for-Tests Anti-Pattern
candidates.description: 74 test files, zero CI enforcement. Tests as documentation, not quality gates. Don't regress on pre-commit enforcement.

candidates.name: Monorepo Without Shared Testing
candidates.description: 35 packages, 0 conftest.py, no shared runner. Plan shared testing if JASON-OS distributes skills as packages.

candidates.name: Growing Faster Than Quality Gates
candidates.description: 28K stars/29d with no automated enforcement. Same trajectory as celebrity stagnation, faster.

security.note: Apache-2.0 license. Python package with dependencies.

documentation.note: Detailed README, HARNESS.md methodology, SKILL.md generation, CLI-Hub site.

process.note: Apache-2.0, CONTRIBUTING.md, active PR merging, 1 release.

velocity.note: 29 days old, 28K stars, daily commits, community contributions.

absencePattern.description: No absence patterns detected. Rapidly growing project with active maintenance.

creatorLens: This repo understands that the next wave of software users will be AI agents, not humans. It provides a systematic methodology (HARNESS.md) for wrapping any desktop application in a CLI that agents can invoke. The 7-phase SOP, SKILL.md generation, and CLI-Hub registry represent a complete ecosystem for agent-native software — directly relevant to JASON-OS's vision of AI-operated workflows.
